underlying: Republican margin of victory, 2026 Kansas U.S. Senate general election (Nov 3, 2026). Incumbent Roger Marshall (R) running for 2nd term.
method: AUDIT FIX 2026-04-29: mu 9->7, sigma 8->10. Anchor: 2020 Marshall beat Bollier ~13pts (54-41 with Lib). 2022 Moran reelected easily. 2024 Trump won KS by ~16pts. Audit caught: Tavern Research poll showed independent Hamilton 51-49 over Marshall -- combined with DDHQ Oct 2025 also showing Hamilton narrowly ahead, indie threat is sustaining beyond a single-poll fluke. Mu compressed to 7, sigma widened to 10 reflecting genuinely competitive race + indie volatility. Modeled R margin ~ N(7, 10).
notes: KS is the most competitive of the 8. Marshall won 2020 by only +13 in a strong R year. KS gov Laura Kelly (D) won statewide. One DDHQ poll showed indie Hamilton narrowly ahead (small sample, single poll). 2024 Trump only +16. Model: μ=9 (between 2020 Marshall +13 and a discount for D-leaning environment + indie challenge). Kalshi YES asks: P3 0.73, P5 0.68, P9 0.59, P11 0.54, P13 0.50, P15 0.45 — implies market median ~13. Our model is slightly bearish vs market (μ=9 vs market ~13) reflecting D+5 generic ballot tailwind and indie threat.
